transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hello, hello.
Hello.
How's it going?
How are you guys doing?
Good.
I'm good.
It's raining here.
Oh, yeah?
Is that normal for this time?
Yeah.
No.
It's normal to have winter in Israel.
don't really have a winter.
It's like...
Yeah.
Never.
It's just more of the same.
Yeah, always like, yeah, usually it's always hot.
Yeah, it's always nice.
Everyone thinks South Africa is like always hot and it's actually, the winters are horrible.
Yeah, it's freezing.
Yeah, it's not nice.
Alrighty, let me pull up my screen.
So first things first, akina, I saw your message just before we dive into like regular update.
saw your message.
Feedback is that we can't piecemeal the engagement.
So it's the flat, it's flat 12,000 for the core package as a whole.
So I just, I wanted to share that in the call.
But we can't look at like anything less than the core package, essentially.
you.
Thank
Okay.
So main thing from our end is we'll need to reduce the budget, the monthly cost somehow.
We can only go up to like 8K moving forward.
So I'm not sure what that looks like on your end.
But basically we're trying to figure out what's the cost and what is creating the 12K.
If we can't itemize it, then maybe you can go back to Andy and the team and figure out like what would make sense and what would need to be shaved off in order to hit that requirement.
Yeah, I'll definitely check with Andy, but I think the feedback is going to be quite clear that we can't negotiate any lower than that 12K package.
That's the core set of offerings and the elements in the engagement all fold into one another.
I will check, but I want to be clear straight away that I don't think there's going to be any room to come down, but I'll see internally and come back to you.
Okay.
And then the other thing, too, is I believe this is an annual renewal.
Correct.

The other thing is we want to see if that is feasible to switch into either a biannual or quarterly renewal, given some of the – I think just looking at last year's performance, there was a lot of ups and downs quarter to quarter, and so we want some flexibility in between those if that were to occur again.
Okay, cool.
I'll add that to the ask, and I'll let you know.
Okay.
Thanks.
Alrighty, is there anything else related to the Renewal Deck or like specifics that you guys wanted to raise or is it just on the pricing and the cadence?
Just the Conjure.
Okay, cool.
Alrighty.
Okay, then let's get into a bit of an update.
We obviously, I think you were out of office and then we talked to Renewal last week, so a bit of a check-in.
Let me share my, here's the content OS.
Okay, so we have, we're working nicely through the Ketamine cluster right now.
We have three refreshes that we targeted left to complete.
Akina, I saw your message about like what we're doing there in the refreshes.
There should be comments in the top to explain like the approach to each individual article, but we're not changing, we're not changing URL path.

So anything like that, it's just an update on the content and improvement, generally improvement, internal linking, a rework on like SERP analysis and identifying some elements for information gain to improve the ranking.
So nothing from like a broken link perspective or anything like that.
And to be clear, I know you did raise that there are some articles that you guys share and didn't want amended.
So those haven't been included in the refresh.
These were targeted because they relate to the ketamine cluster.
They were published before we kind of reworked the strategy and we saw some opportunity to go and update based on the work we've done.
So nothing for you to stress about there.
In terms of in production, so we've got a couple of verses.
I did want to just like double check these with you because...
They've been flagged in the past, so ketamine versus Xanax.
There's volume here.
Just want to make sure before we submit to you that these are all good.
Yeah, they're all These topics are fine.
Okay.
Okay, that's awesome.
Okay, so like I said, working nicely through the ketamine cluster, we have a fair few more to start.
But what I had been doing additionally is looking back through like what I'm calling general mental health topics.
There's probably a better term for this, but it's just a label at the moment.
If we like go back and look at some of the top performing content for Innerwell, so we've got quite a few like ketamine type pieces that we've produced recently.
But some of like some of the topics that have really stuck around and continue to perform are this type.
So emotional safety boundaries, fear of happiness, how to heal.
These are just continuing to, like, perform very well.
So I went and did a bit of content strategy around, like, are there potentially more topics like this that we can go and grab and double down on some of this performance?
So in Airtable, under Considering, we've loaded 51 new assignments for these general mental health topics.
You don't need to go through all of them at once, but a bit of homework for you.
Could you take a look in here and either, like, give me the OK or call out a couple, or if you'd like to, you can just change the status in here to Approve to Start.
I just want your eyes on it because we did this for the Ketamine Therapy Cluster, and as these are kind of new topics, I just want to make sure we're alive.
But there's a ton of volume and based on our performance in the past on these topics, then I think we'd be, we'd definitely be missing some opportunities here if we're not doing this.
So question coming out of that, like, ketamine was a key target cluster for us and we talked about expanding into the other type of treatments you guys offer.
But how do you feel generally about this kind of topic?
Because it's not so, like, cluster treatment specific, let's say.
Yeah, these are more, like, surface, if not, not quite condition based.
These are, are these all for all the surfaces or just for specific ones?
Yeah, I suppose it would be, like, for all.
And then depending on how, like, the content production goes, perhaps there will be some where we can.
the content
And think about linking through to a specific cluster and trying to use that as part of the funnel.
But I think generally this would be across all services as whole.
And then the conversion pathway through to general mental health landing pages.
But I think there will be different opportunities throughout.
It will be very topic dependent, right?
But that would be the idea, definitely.
So, yeah, like I said, the main idea here is like this kind of content has performed very well in the past for Innerwell.
So I think we'd be missing a trick.
We didn't consider these again.
Okay, I'll take a look at those.
Cool.
Okay, that's great.
Okay, so let's take a look while we're on performance.
So this is kind of a January check-in.
Okay.
This is just looking at editorial pages.
So pages we published on Reflections, growth is looking excellent.
Started the month at around 630 sessions a week.
And this last week, we essentially doubled overall sessions to the blog.
So that's super encouraging.
Engagement is still continuing up.
I want to get a little bit higher, but the engagement rate, you can see, like, across some of our top blogs, it's, like, really healthy, 60%, 70%.
We'll take learnings from some of the lower engaged pieces and think about, as we expand out into, like, new treatment topics.
But really, like, the benefit of getting five out a week is that we get to make a lot of shots on goal and learn where our sweet spot is, again, relating back to these.
Mental health style topics.
Mental
But, like, super encouraging overall, I think, continuing to grow week on week.
Yeah, some of our top blogs.
This is a piece that we wrote a couple of weeks ago, Ketamine Laws by State, which is, like, a reference guide.
And then, again, some of our, like, I guess, OG pieces here that are continuing to perform really well.
So in terms of PSEO, looking good.
We're definitely seeing that, like, a continued impact of all the redirect work we did.
So if we look at Mental Health States, you know, these ones.
Yeah, so we can see, like, overall sessions are continuing to grow across these landing pages.
Super encouraging.
encouraging.
And then this image here, this shows us that, again, everything is moving across nicely.
So engagement, we need to get up, definitely, and that's something that we lost when we broke the URLs.
But we'll be keeping an eye on this to see that engagement moves up.
But generally, the message here is traffic is moving back to those landing pages and back at pre the URL break.
Levels, so super encouraging.
Georgia, California, Texas are among the top states.
So PSEO is looking really good.
I do have a question around conversion.
We raised it a little bit in the, well, firstly, just on performance.
Is there anything you guys want to, like, want me to dive into or take a deeper look out there?
That was my exact question, was if we can look at
Yeah, okay, so there's a couple of things that will need your help on setting up your GA4.
So the way that we're tracking conversions through Looker at the moment, I've taken a peek here, and essentially on, let's just bring up a, so.
First thing is, I'll put this in, and I'll put this in a recap so you have this written down, but like, so this, this CTA here right now is not tagged as a specific conversion event.
So not just from like, growthx blogs, but like at all, so a click on here is currently pulling through as user engagement.
Also, we need to add UTM, UTM to those burners.
We remember what we about.
Yeah.
Yeah, so the problem here is that this click here is being tracked as user engagement.
Now, we are seeing engagement grow, which is encouraging, but this engagement refers to any click on the blog.
So this click through to another page is being tracked in the same way as this click.
So this would need to be reassigned as, so there are a couple parts, like either you can assign these events specific to growthx URLs.

Like I think we were talking about that on some of the landing pages, but generally we need to create a unique event for the CTA because otherwise we can't see the difference between someone arriving and clicking on an internal link versus someone clicking to, to take the assessment.
The other option would be looking at...
Screenery starts, right?
Yes.
Yes, I suppose that.
I'm just not sure.
I have to see.
I have to ask our analogistics guys then if that's an equal alternative.
I would like to see.
I think the easiest thing here would be.
We'll tag it, but in the meantime, I think the session starts would be the equal if not opposite.
And is purchases tagged in here?
Purchases.
Yeah, purchases here.
I think we need to double check.
I don't know if you're like how clean your...
J4 is looking on your side, or whether you guys have devoted some time there cleaning up, but it would be good to get a better understanding, because I'm not clear that purchases are.
Yeah, he's been calling directly from J4, they should be the same.
Yeah, okay, well, so can you maybe take a double check in there and just make sure that's set up correctly?
I'll find out internally whether there's, like, another option for us to track this, because I know we said, like, okay, do we want to put specific growthx?
Do we want to attribute specifically to growthx pages as well?
Let me dig into that a little bit, and I'll come back with some recommendations.
think the first thing was to just identify, like, what's going on, what is J4 currently doing?
And then you guys are...
But you're using some other tools, I think, as well, correct?
Or is it just GA4?
Yeah, we use Mixpanel.
Okay, yeah, cool.
Is there a way for us to get, like, some regular readouts from Mixpanel across, like, the editorial pages, especially?
Yeah, I think I could probably schedule a report to go to.
That'd be great.

But, yeah, I think as we focus more on conversion this year and where these, you know, how do we create great traffic is increasing, like, performance looking good, let's start looking at conversions.
And I just want to make sure we're looking at the same thing you guys are and clear on kind of the goals we're working to do.
So I'll do a little bit of digging on my side to see what's possible.
And then if you find anything interesting on your end in GA4, then...
Please let me know.
Cool.
That's the only ask.
I don't have anything else to share with you right now.
Is there anything on your mind, like, production-wise that you wanted to talk about?
No.
I think this makes sense.
This looks good.
Just the contract stuff is obviously most sensitive.
No problem.
Cool.
Okay.
I'll try and get you some clarity, like, today, if I can.
And I'll let you know.
I'll let you know about the blog, Ted.
Cool.
Okay.
All right.
Let's give you guys some time back.
Okay.
Thanks, Matt.
All right, guys.
Take care.
Bye.
Ciao.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review renewal terms and January content/performance.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=88.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Renewal Blocked:** Innerwell's budget is capped at $8k/mo, but GrowthX's core package is a non-negotiable $12k/mo. Innerwell also requested flexible renewal terms (quarterly/biannual).](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=88.0)
  - [**Traffic Doubled:** January blog traffic doubled to \~1,260 sessions/week, driven by the Ketamine cluster and successful PSEO landing page redirects.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
  - [**Conversion Tracking Broken:** GA4 fails to distinguish CTA clicks from general page engagement, making it impossible to measure content ROI.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)
  - [**New Strategy Proposed:** A new "general mental health" content cluster (51 topics) was proposed to replicate the success of high-performing evergreen articles.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)

## Topics

### Renewal & Budget

  - [**Innerwell Request:** Reduce monthly budget to $8k.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=121.0)
  - [**GrowthX Position:** The $12k/mo core package is non-negotiable and cannot be itemized.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=88.0)
  - [**Innerwell Request:** Switch from annual to quarterly or biannual renewal for flexibility, citing past performance volatility.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=197.0)
  - [**Action:** Matt will present these requests to Andy but set expectations that a budget reduction is unlikely.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=121.0)

### Content Strategy & Production

  - [**Ketamine Cluster:** Nearing completion with 3 articles left to refresh.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=270.0)
      - [**Refreshes:** Content updates only (SERP analysis, internal linking) → no URL changes.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
      - [**Exclusions:** Articles Innerwell uses for sharing were intentionally excluded.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)
  - [**New Strategy: "General Mental Health" Cluster**](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Replicate the success of evergreen articles (e.g., "emotional safety") that consistently perform well.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
      - [**Proposal:** 51 new topic assignments added to Airtable for Innerwell review.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
      - [**Goal:** Drive traffic to general mental health landing pages, with potential for specific service funnels.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)

### Performance & Tracking

  - [**January Traffic:**](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
      - [**Blog:** Doubled traffic to \~1,260 sessions/week.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
      - [**Top Performers:** "Ketamine Laws by State" and evergreen articles.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [**Engagement:** Strong on top blogs (60–70% rate); learnings from lower-performing pieces will inform future strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
  - [**PSEO Landing Pages:**](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=687.0)
      - [**Redirects:** Successfully restored traffic to pre-URL-break levels.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=695.0)
      - [**Top States:** Georgia, California, Texas.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=754.0)
      - [**Challenge:** Engagement on these pages remains low post-redirect and requires improvement.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [**Conversion Tracking Issue:**](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)
      - [**Problem:** The "Take the Assessment" CTA click is tracked as "user engagement" in GA4.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)
      - [**Impact:** This event is indistinguishable from any other click on the page, preventing accurate ROI measurement for content.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
      - [**Solution:** Create a unique, dedicated conversion event for the CTA.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)
  - [**Mixpanel:** Innerwell also uses Mixpanel; a recurring report on editorial pages was requested to align on conversion metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Matt:**](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=121.0)
      - [Present renewal requests ($8k budget, flexible terms) to Andy.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=121.0)
      - [Research and recommend a GA4 event structure for tracking CTA clicks.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)
  - [**Akina:**](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0)
      - [Review the 51 new "general mental health" topics in Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0)
      - [Verify GA4 "purchase" event data accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=942.0)
      - [Set up a recurring Mixpanel report for editorial pages.](https://fathom.video/share/C5U6GKAv5CSzaSegN9B1ZgT6baTc4h1z?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)

action_items.description: Confirm w/ Andy re: $8k/mo budget; report back to Akina/Noa
Confirm w/ Andy re: biannual/quarterly renewal; report back to Akina/Noa
Review 51 general mental health topics in Airtable; approve/select for Matt
Implement GA4 tracking: tag blog CTA; add UTMs; verify purchases; send to Matt
Consult GrowthX analytics re: GA4 CTA/attribution; send recs to Akina/Noa
Schedule Mixpanel report for editorial pages to GrowthX
